channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Arts-led program offers trauma-informed and cultural sensitivity training in Saskatoon
item.description: A new initiative in Saskatoon is aiming to foster greater understanding of trauma and cultural sensitivity through arts-based education. The Community Arts Mentorship Program has introduced Trauma-Inf...

item.title: Femi Azeez targets Premier League move after Super Eagles debut brace
item.description: Femi Azeez, Millwall winger and Super Eagles forward, has openly expressed his ambition to play in the English Premier League after an impressive start to his international career. The 24-year-old mad...

item.title: Kerry-Lynne Findlay elected leader of BC Conservative Party after close leadership race
item.description: Kerry-Lynne Findlay has been elected leader of the British Columbia Conservative Party following a closely contested leadership race that was decided through a ranked ballot system. Findlay secured 4,...

item.title: Rains expected in parts of the Philippines due to Typhoon Domeng and habagat
item.description: The Philippines is expected to experience widespread rains on Sunday, May 31, as a combination of Typhoon Domeng’s trough and the southwest monsoon, locally called habagat, affects several regions. As...

item.title: Iran conflict-driven cost surge threatens tourism recovery across Southeast Asia
item.description: Rising geopolitical tensions linked to the war in Iran are placing significant strain on the tourism sector across Southeast Asia, a region still recovering from the economic impacts of the COVID-19 p...

item.title: Architect Mfundo Maphumulo on enduring design, simplicity and the social role of public architecture in South Africa
item.description: In this reflective column-style interview held at Johannesburg’s historic Rand Club, architect Mfundo Maphumulo shares his philosophy on architecture, life and the long-term impact of design. The conv...

item.title: Parliament Faces Scrutiny Over Secretary's Salary and Constitutional Compliance
item.description: The National Assembly debated the 2026/27 budget amid concerns over parliamentary autonomy and the fallout from the Phala Phala judgment. Speaker Thoko Didiza highlighted that some parliamentary rules...

item.title: Two injured in Highway 401 crash in Mississauga
item.description: A collision on Highway 401 in Mississauga resulted in two people being taken to hospital following the incident, according to reporting published by CTV News Toronto. The crash occurred on the evening...

item.title: PSG earns over €100 million following UEFA Champions League title defence
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) has added another major achievement to their history after successfully defending their UEFA Champions League title with a dramatic victory over Arsenal in Budapest. The matc...

item.title: Escalation continues in Israel-Iran tensions as Lebanon condemns strikes and US signals readiness amid stalled nuclear deal talks
item.description: The live coverage reports a continued escalation in West Asia involving Israel, Iran, and regional actors. Lebanon’s Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has strongly condemned Israel’s military operations in s...

item.title: Mika Salamanca shares her thoughts on working with Will Ashley in upcoming projects
item.description: In a recent interview, popular Filipino actress Mika Salamanca addressed fan speculation about a possible collaboration with fellow celebrity Will Ashley. Many enthusiasts have been eager to see the t...

item.title: Analysis finds flaws in Tesla’s safety claims and limits of its self-driving technology
item.description: This report examines how Tesla presents safety and performance claims about its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system, raising concerns from researchers and former employees. Tesla has promoted statements th...

item.title: New Blood Test Shows Promise in Early Detection of Multiple Cancers
item.description: A recent NHS-backed trial has shown that the Galleri blood test, developed by American firm Grail, could revolutionise early cancer detection. The test identifies fragments of cancerous DNA circulatin...

item.title: Key Political, Economic and Sports Developments Across Nigeria and Abroad on 31st May 2026
item.description: Today’s Nigerian newspaper roundup captures major developments across politics, economy, security, sports, and entertainment. In the economy, Dangote Petroleum Refinery announced a reduction in petrol...

item.title: Woman dies in early-morning collision in Birmingham city centre, man arrested
item.description: A 28-year-old woman tragically lost her life after being struck by a black Audi RSQ8 on Dartmouth Middleway in Birmingham city centre shortly before 2:30am. The incident led to the closure of the A454...

item.title: Trump could maintain influence over the Republican Party without seeking another term
item.description: A Republican strategist, Liam Donavan, shared his perspective on former President Donald Trump's ongoing influence within the GOP, emphasizing that Trump may not pursue a third term but could still ex...

item.title: Regina symposium focuses on youth education around addiction, recovery, and mental health
item.description: A community event in Regina brought together youth, educators, and health professionals to raise awareness about addiction, recovery, and mental health. The initiative aimed to provide young people wi...

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Show Progress, But Tensions Remain
item.description: In Singapore, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth indicated that while negotiations with Iran over ending the recent conflict have been productive, the possibility of military action remains on the tabl...

item.title: Climate Activists Protest TotalEnergies Over Fossil Fuel Projects in Africa
item.description: On 29 May 2026, climate activists gathered outside TotalEnergies’ Johannesburg offices to protest the company’s fossil fuel projects in East Africa, Mozambique, Namibia, and South Africa. The demonstr...

item.title: Kerry-Lynne Findlay elected leader of BC Conservatives ahead of next provincial election
item.description: Kerry-Lynne Findlay, a lawyer and former federal minister of national revenue under former prime minister Stephen Harper, has been elected leader of the British Columbia Conservative Party. She secure...
